checks.cmd: python3 -m py_compile scripts/workflow_validate.py scripts/workflow_validate_core.py scripts/workflow_checks.py scripts/workflow_checks_core.py scripts/workflow_detect.py scripts/workflow_utils.py scripts/workflow_render.py scripts/workflow_hooks.py scripts/workflow_memory.py
checks.name: lint

principleNotes: Correctness: 2/2 — Logic verified, 265 tests pass, edge cases handled (empty graph, min_size, convergence)
Security: 2/2 — No secrets, no subprocess calls in new code, no injection risk
Contract Compliance: 2/2 — All artifacts present, lifecycle correct, workers report-done only
Performance: 2/2 — O(N*E*iterations) acceptable, bulk query, early convergence, max 100 iterations
Maintainability: 2/2 — Follows coupling.py pattern exactly, clean separation
Test Coverage: 2/2 — 16 new tests (8 community, 3 graph, 5 CLI) covering happy/error/edge cases
Scope Discipline: 2/2 — Surgical changes, plan-scoped only, no drive-by edits

evidence: All 3 plan tasks implemented: community.py algorithm, ContextGraph.communities() method, CLI graph-communities
Only plan-scoped files modified (no drive-by edits)
Phase progression: discuss -> plan -> implement -> review
stageReviews.notes: Scope matches plan goal exactly. 16 new tests across 3 test files.

evidence: Label propagation uses sorted iteration for determinism, Counter for voting, min() tie-breaking
CLI handler follows try/finally pattern for graph.close()
All new code follows existing patterns in coupling.py and workflow_memory.py
stageReviews.notes: Clean implementation. No security, performance, or maintainability concerns.
